entities.name: OpenAI
entities.blurb: Maker of ChatGPT and the GPT models. Restructured into OpenAI Group PBC (a public-benefit corporation) in October 2025, controlled by the non-profit OpenAI Foundation.

entities.name: Anthropic
entities.blurb: Maker of the Claude models and a public-benefit corporation. Backed heavily by Amazon and Google while remaining independently controlled by its founders and a long-term-benefit trust.

entities.name: Google DeepMind
entities.blurb: Google's unified AI research lab (DeepMind + Google Brain), maker of the Gemini models. A wholly-owned part of Alphabet.

entities.name: Meta Superintelligence Labs
entities.blurb: Meta's AI organization (incorporating FAIR and the Llama models), reorganized in 2025 under Alexandr Wang to pursue superintelligence.

entities.name: xAI
entities.blurb: Maker of Grok. Merged with X (formerly Twitter) in March 2025, then was absorbed by SpaceX in early 2026 in a combined ~$1.25T entity controlled by Elon Musk.

entities.name: Mistral AI
entities.blurb: France's leading AI lab, known for open-weight models. Europe's flagship independent frontier effort.

entities.name: DeepSeek
entities.blurb: Chinese lab whose efficient open-weight models shook the market in early 2025. Funded and controlled by quant hedge fund High-Flyer.

entities.name: Safe Superintelligence (SSI)
entities.blurb: Founded by ex-OpenAI chief scientist Ilya Sutskever to build safe superintelligence directly, with no interim products.

entities.name: Thinking Machines Lab
entities.blurb: Founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, with a number of senior OpenAI researchers.

entities.name: Cohere
entities.blurb: Enterprise-focused foundation-model company built for business and RAG workloads.

entities.name: Perplexity AI
entities.blurb: AI-native answer engine / search product.

entities.name: Character.AI
entities.blurb: Consumer chatbot / companion platform. In 2024 Google licensed its technology and re-hired co-founder Noam Shazeer.

entities.name: Inflection AI
entities.blurb: Maker of the Pi assistant. In March 2024 Microsoft hired most of its team, including co-founder Mustafa Suleyman, in an acqui-hire; Inflection pivoted to enterprise.

entities.name: Hugging Face
entities.blurb: The open-source hub and platform for sharing models and datasets.

entities.name: Stability AI
entities.blurb: Maker of Stable Diffusion image models.

entities.name: ElevenLabs
entities.blurb: Leading AI voice / speech synthesis company.

entities.name: Midjourney
entities.blurb: AI image generator. Notably bootstrapped and profitable — it has taken no outside investment.

entities.name: Scale AI
entities.blurb: Data-labeling and evaluation infrastructure for AI training. Meta took a 49% non-voting stake in 2025.

entities.name: Microsoft
entities.blurb: Largest OpenAI shareholder; also runs its own MAI models and absorbed most of Inflection's team.

entities.name: Alphabet / Google
entities.blurb: Owns Google DeepMind outright and is a major backer of Anthropic; licensed Character.AI's technology.

entities.name: Amazon
entities.blurb: Anthropic's largest investor and primary cloud/compute partner (AWS, Trainium).

entities.name: Meta
entities.blurb: Runs Meta Superintelligence Labs and holds a 49% non-voting stake in Scale AI.

entities.name: Nvidia
entities.blurb: The dominant AI-chip maker and a prolific strategic investor across the AI stack.

entities.name: SpaceX
entities.blurb: Elon Musk's rocket company, which absorbed xAI in early 2026.

entities.name: ASML
entities.blurb: The Dutch monopoly supplier of EUV lithography machines; lead investor in Mistral.

entities.name: Salesforce
entities.blurb: Enterprise-software giant and strategic investor via Salesforce Ventures.

entities.name: SoftBank
entities.blurb: Japanese investment holding company; a lead backer of OpenAI's mega-rounds.

entities.name: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z)
entities.blurb: Silicon Valley venture firm with one of the broadest AI portfolios.

entities.name: Thrive Capital
entities.blurb: Josh Kushner's venture firm; a lead investor in OpenAI.

entities.name: Greenoaks Capital
entities.blurb: Growth investor; lead backer of Safe Superintelligence.

entities.name: High-Flyer
entities.blurb: Chinese quantitative hedge fund that founded, funds and controls DeepSeek.

entities.name: OpenAI Foundation
entities.blurb: The non-profit that controls OpenAI Group PBC and holds roughly a 26% economic interest.

relationships.detail: The non-profit OpenAI Foundation governs OpenAI Group PBC and holds ~26% of the equity after the October 2025 for-profit restructuring.
Microsoft holds ~27% (26.79% fully diluted) of OpenAI Group PBC after the restructuring, plus IP/model access through 2032. The exclusive partnership and revenue-share ended in April 2026.

sources.title: Microsoft to get 27% of OpenAI — Bloomberg
OpenAI and Microsoft end exclusive partnership — Forbes

relationships.detail: SoftBank led OpenAI's record 2025–2026 financing rounds, which lifted the company's valuation past $800B.
Thrive Capital is among OpenAI's largest venture backers and has co-led recent rounds.

Amazon is Anthropic's largest investor and primary cloud partner. Its position (convertible notes + non-voting preferred) was reported worth ~$74B on paper by mid-2026, with commitments to invest much more.

sources.title: Amazon and Google's billions riding on Anthropic — Fortune

relationships.detail: In April 2026 Google committed up to $40B to Anthropic ($10B wired immediately at a ~$350B valuation), on top of earlier multi-billion investments. Google holds a minority, non-controlling stake.

sources.title: Amazon and Google's billions riding on Anthropic — Fortune

relationships.detail: Google DeepMind is a wholly-owned division of Alphabet, formed by merging DeepMind and Google Brain in 2023.
Meta Superintelligence Labs is a wholly-owned Meta organization, reorganized in 2025 under Alexandr Wang.

sources.title: Meta takes 49% stake in Scale AI, Wang joins — CDO Magazine

relationships.detail: Meta paid ~$14.3B for a 49% non-voting stake in Scale AI (valuing it ~$29B) and brought founder Alexandr Wang into Meta. Scale continues to operate independently.

sources.title: Scale AI founder Wang exits for Meta — CNBC
Meta takes 49% stake in Scale AI — CDO Magazine

relationships.detail: SpaceX absorbed xAI in early 2026 in the largest merger on record, a combined ~$1.25T entity controlled by Elon Musk. xAI had merged with X (Twitter) in March 2025.

sources.title: Musk's xAI, SpaceX combo biggest merger ever — CNBC
SpaceX combines with xAI at $1.25T — Bloomberg

relationships.detail: ASML led Mistral's €1.7B Series C in September 2025 with ~€1.3B, taking an ~11% stake (its largest shareholder) and a seat on Mistral's strategic committee. Valuation ~€11.7B.

sources.title: Mistral valued at $14B as ASML takes major stake — CNBC

relationships.detail: Nvidia is a recurring investor in Mistral, participating in its Series B and Series C rounds.
DeepSeek was founded and is funded and controlled by the Chinese quant hedge fund High-Flyer; it has taken no external venture capital.
Greenoaks led financing for Safe Superintelligence, which has raised at a multi-billion-dollar valuation despite having no product.
Andreessen Horowitz is among the venture backers of Safe Superintelligence.
Andreessen Horowitz led Thinking Machines Lab's very large seed round in 2025.
Nvidia is a strategic investor in Cohere.
Salesforce Ventures is a long-standing strategic investor in Cohere.
Nvidia is among Perplexity's strategic investors.
SoftBank has invested in Perplexity via its Vision Fund.
In August 2024 Google struck a ~$2.7B deal to license Character.AI's technology and re-hire co-founder Noam Shazeer and other staff, while Character.AI stayed independent.

In March 2024 Microsoft hired co-founders Mustafa Suleyman and Karén Simonyan plus most of Inflection's staff to form Microsoft AI, paying a licensing fee; Inflection continued as an enterprise company.
Salesforce led Hugging Face's Series D; the round also included Google, Amazon, Nvidia and others.
Nvidia participated in Hugging Face's Series D financing.
Andreessen Horowitz has led financing rounds for ElevenLabs.
